prepositions_phrasal_verbs_prepositions_.name: prepositions phrasal verbs prepositions of time all levels

questions.question: Choose the preposition for exact time: The lesson starts {a1} nine o’clock.
Choose the preposition for a day: We have a meeting {a1} Monday.
Choose the preposition for a month: My birthday is {a1} April.
Choose the expression: I study {a1} the evening.
Choose the time relation: Please call me {a1} lunch.
Choose the preposition before a noun: I slept {a1} the flight.
Choose the preposition for duration: She has lived here {a1} three years.
Choose the deadline preposition: Send the report {a1} Friday.
Choose the range expression: The shop is open {a1} nine to five.
Choose the fixed expression: I usually read {a1} night.
Avoid the mistake: The train leaves {a1} Tuesday morning.
Choose the starting point preposition: I have known her {a1} 2020.
